title: GOOD Graph Out-of-Distribution Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Use official loaders to recover split metadata and domain selections.

instructions: Install or inspect the GOOD package and choose one graph dataset/domain selection.
Use official split metadata as operating points, then build SPECTRA curves around graph similarity or domain support.
Record graph task type, domain selection, covariate/concept shift label, and random seed.

scale.download_size: variable by graph dataset; begin with one official dataset/domain selection

recommended_axes.name: graph_structural_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Graph kernel, WL-feature, substructure, or learned graph-embedding similarity.

recommended_axes.name: domain_support
recommended_axes.definition: Distance from training domain labels or domain-feature centroids.

recommended_axes.name: local_neighborhood_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Node or ego-graph neighborhood overlap for node-level tasks.

useful_for: Auditing graph model generalization beyond biology and chemistry.
Testing whether benchmark OOD splits sample one point on a broader graph novelty curve.
Comparing covariate and concept shift behavior in SPECTRA terms.
leakage_risks: Node-level tasks can leak through graph connectivity if train/test masking is ignored.
Domain labels may be too close to the official split definition and should be contextualized as operating points.
Learned graph embeddings must not be fitted on held-out labels.
